metadata.title: Kokoro: Free Voices (With Names)
metadata.purpose: Video for 'Brutalist — Claude for Video Production' on @NikBearBrown. Introduces the THIRD voice engine: Kokoro-82M via kokoro-onnx (generate_audio_kokoro.py) — free, local, Apache 2.0, ~28 named preset voices with the pack's own quality grades. THE CAST CARRIES THE VIDEO: B00 opens in Bear's ElevenLabs clone, then hands off to eight named Kokoro voices — Bella (A-), Sarah (C+), Adam (F+, the honest-record beat), Michael (C+), Emma (B-), George (C), Puck (C+), Santa (D-) — each introducing themselves, each beat cast via the sheet's per-beat 'voice' field. Teaches: same-interface plug, the casting field, the roster + grades, previz use (greybox for the ears), the no-cloning tradeoff, and the three-engine split (metered/flat/free). Grades verified against hexgrad/Kokoro-82M VOICES.md on 2026-07-13 — re-verify at build (FACTCHECK).
metadata.clock: narration — the master clock; every beat conforms to its measured audio (any engine)

checklist: no API · no meter · no bill

beats.narration_text: This is my voice — the ElevenLabs clone that's narrated every video in this series. It's good. It's also metered. So for the rest of this video, I'm handing the microphone to a cast that costs exactly nothing. They live on this laptop. And they have names.

production_viz.label: Bella · grade A- · what Kokoro is
production_viz.mechanic: A roster name card, top-billed: BELLA, grade chip A- in teal. Beside it the model card: Kokoro-82M — 82 million parameters, Apache 2.0, runs LOCALLY (a laptop icon, no cloud). Three crossed-out items in red: API, meter, bill. Caption: 'what free sounds like.'

beats.narration_text: I'm Bella. I'm a Kokoro voice — an eighty-two-million-parameter open model, Apache licensed, running locally on the same machine that's rendering these graphics. No A P I. No meter. No bill. I'm what free sounds like.

production_viz.label: Sarah · C+ · the voice is a plug
production_viz.mechanic: Name card: SARAH, grade chip C+. The same-interface diagram from the cost test, extended: THREE engines (ElevenLabs metered / Suno flat / Kokoro FREE) all feeding the identical row of slots — mp3/beat-*.mp3 with measured durations — and one unbroken teal pipeline underneath (render · captions · shorts · publish). Caption: 'downstream never knows.'

beats.narration_text: I'm Sarah. Here's why we plug straight in: a voice engine in this toolkit is just something that fills one mp3 per beat and measures it. Same files, same durations, same beat sheet. Downstream, nothing knows I'm not the expensive one.

production_viz.label: Adam · F+ · the honest record
production_viz.mechanic: Name card: ADAM, grade chip F+ in red — styled like a school report card. Around it, the pack's own grading scale A→F with a few example voices plotted. The card is NOT hidden or softened; it is the beat. Caption: 'the pack grades its own voices — and it doesn't flatter.'

beats.narration_text: I'm Adam. On the official report card, I'm graded F plus — the pack grades its own voices, and it doesn't flatter us. They let me say this beat anyway. That's the honest record: free has a range, and you should hear the bottom of it.

output: change the field, change the reader.

beats.narration_text: I'm Michael. The command is one line — and the casting is one field. This very beat says voice: A M underscore Michael. Change the field, change the reader. That's the whole workflow.

production_viz.label: Emma · B- · the roster
production_viz.mechanic: Name card: EMMA, grade chip B-, with a small Union Jack tick (British English). Behind it the English roster grid: 28 voice tiles (20 American af_/am_, 8 British bf_/bm_), each with its grade chip A- to F+, the four heard-so-far tiles highlighted. A footer strip lists the wider pack: 54 voices · 9 languages (JA 5 · ZH 8 · ES 3 · FR 1 · HI 4 · IT 2 · PT 3) with an honest small-print note: 'non-English support is thinner.' Caption: 'casting is taste — and taste is free.'

beats.narration_text: I'm Emma — one of the British voices. There are twenty-eight of us in English: twenty American, eight British, graded A to F by the pack itself. And the wider pack speaks nine languages — fifty-four voices in all. You've just heard an A minus, a C plus, and an F plus. Casting is taste — and now taste is free.

production_viz.label: George · C · greybox for the ears
production_viz.mechanic: Name card: GEORGE, grade chip C. A two-stage flow: DRAFT (Kokoro, $0) → real measured durations → conforms → watchable review cut → THEN the paid voice pass re-fills the same slots (ElevenLabs/Suno icons, unlocked last). Caption: 'spend taste first, credits last.'

beats.narration_text: I'm George. The strongest use isn't the final cut — it's the draft. Free narration means real timings, real conforms, a watchable preview, before a single paid credit is spent. Greybox — for the ears.

production_viz.label: Puck · C+ · the catch
production_viz.mechanic: Name card: PUCK, grade chip C+. The tradeoff balance: LEFT (teal) 'free · 28 voices · local · instant'; RIGHT (red) 'no cloning — none of us is YOU'. A small identity mark: Bear's silhouette ≠ any roster tile. Caption: 'when the video is your name, the voice is yours.'

beats.narration_text: I'm Puck. Now the catch, honestly: none of us is you. Kokoro doesn't clone. When the video carries your name and your channel, the voice should be yours — metered or flat. When it doesn't, we work free.

production_viz.label: Santa · D- · the wink
production_viz.mechanic: Name card: SANTA, grade chip D- in red, tiny holly sprig — played completely straight in the house style, which is the joke. Caption: 'you get what you pay for — except you didn't pay.'

beats.narration_text: And I'm Santa. Graded D minus. Ho ho. You get what you pay for — except you didn't pay.

production_viz.label: the roster is three engines now
production_viz.mechanic: The hero beat — most care. Dark bg #2A1A0E, EB Garamond. Three columns: METERED (ElevenLabs — the clone, steadiest, priced by the character), FLAT (Suno — your voice, one generation per video), FREE (Kokoro — the named cast, $0). Under all three, one line in teal: 'WHO SPEAKS IS TASTE — AND TASTE IS YOURS.'

beats.narration_text: So the roster is three engines now. Metered — the clone, steadiest, priced by the character. Flat — your voice through Suno, one generation per video. And free — us. The machine will voice every beat either way. Who speaks is taste, and taste is yours.
That's the free voice option. Subscribe to follow the series, and find the whole toolkit at www dot brutalist dot art. You bring the taste; the machine brings the hands — and now, a cast.
